sound_type: aggressive male splashing in shallow spawning riffles
ecosystem: small stream (spawning), small tributaries (juvenile rearing), open ocean
season: fall, September-November (peak October in most Puget Sound systems)

taxonomy.note: Species 2 in the species-specific Salmonidae chapter (after Chinook at 237)

biology.stream_habitat: More tolerant of smaller streams than Chinook. Uses first and second-order tributaries, beaver ponds.
biology.juvenile_rearing: 1-2 years in freshwater small streams; beaver ponds as winter refugia
biology.spawning_coloration: Ocean: bright silver. Spawning male: dark red-green sides, green head, hooked kype
biology.male_competition: Aggressive -- chasing, biting, body collision for redd access and female access

sound_profile.description: Male competition in shallow riffles: irregular, continuous splashing from chasing, fighting, and body collision. Female redd-digging tail-slap also present. E=6: continuous lighter chaos vs. Chinook episodic heavy impact.
sound_profile.shallow_water_amplification: Shallow riffles (15-45 cm) prevent splash energy absorption -- sound translates outward more than in deeper water
sound_profile.energy_note: Same E=6 as Chinook leap but completely different acoustic texture: continuous irregular vs. episodic heavy

6ppd_quinone.compound: 6PPD-quinone -- transformation product of tire rubber antiozonant 6PPD
6ppd_quinone.mechanism: DHODH enzyme inhibition in Coho (species-specific vulnerability). LC50 ~0.8 micrograms/liter
6ppd_quinone.mortality_rates: Up to 90% pre-spawn mortality in heavily urbanized Puget Sound tributaries
6ppd_quinone.policy_response: Washington State SB 5804 (2023) -- first tire rubber chemistry legislation for wildlife protection worldwide
6ppd_quinone.species_specificity: Chinook and Rainbow Trout less sensitive; Coho uniquely vulnerable

esa_status: Puget Sound Coho ESU: Species of Concern
recovery: beaver reintroduction supports Coho juvenile habitat

theory_nodes.concept: Coho shallow-riffle spawning sound: E=6, continuous chaotic splashing in 15-45 cm water. Shallow water amplifies sound by preventing downward energy absorption. Male competition produces irregular high-frequency splashing unlike any other salmon species' spawning sound.
theory_nodes.description: The acoustic physics of Coho spawning in shallow riffles.

theory_nodes.concept: 6PPD-quinone: tire rubber antiozonant breakdown product discovered 2020 as cause of urban Coho pre-spawn mortality. Species-specific toxicity (DHODH mechanism). Up to 90% mortality in urbanized streams. First-ever tire chemistry legislation in Washington (2023).
theory_nodes.description: The most important Coho conservation discovery of the 2020s.

theory_nodes.concept: Beaver-Coho ecology link: beaver ponds create the slow, deep water essential for Coho juvenile winter rearing. Restore beavers = restore Coho. SPS 61-62 American Beaver connects to SPS 238 Coho survival.
theory_nodes.description: Beaver pond habitat as critical Coho juvenile rearing environment.

cross_references.relationship: Coho introduced in genus overview. 6PPD-quinone mentioned. Degree 238 provides full documentation.
Previous Salmonidae species. E=6 same energy; completely different acoustic character. Episodic heavy impact vs. continuous chaotic splashing.
Next Salmonidae species. Winter-run, anadromous rainbow trout. Different season, different river type, same family.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=6 equilibrium. Coho aggressive spawning chaos + PUSA alt-pop comedic energy. Both are E=6, both are energetic and non-serious in the conventional sense, both are fundamentally vital and alive. E=6+6=12.
